case_title: EDITIONS LIMITED WEST, INC. VS. SOMERSET STUDIOS INCORPORATED ET AL

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS SOMERSET STUDIOS INCORPORATED AND STEPHEN WEST'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 12, 2015 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE RESPONDENT THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. RESPONDENT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE NOTICE OF PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD AND RESPONSE IN OPPOSITION TO PETITION TO CORRECT (FILED ON SEPTEMBER 26, 2014) TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 8, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE PETITION TO CORRECT FILED BY PETITIONER ON AUGUST 28, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulation To Maintain Status Quo
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SACHA EMANUEL, ESQ. of Isaacman, Kaufman & Painter, Tel No. 310-881-6800 appearing telephonically for the Petitioner;
Atty. ROBERT E. WHITE, ESQ. of Law Offices of Robert E. White, Tel No. 415-788-6151 appearing for Respondents Somerset Studios Incorporated and Stephen West.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Respondents Somerset Studios Incorporated and Stephen West's motion to enforce the stipulated order filed December 2, 2015 is denied. The absence of any language in that document referring to an appeal of a decision made by this court on a motion seeking to set aside the levy is a strong indication that such an appeal, which likely would not be decided any sooner than the previously filed appeal by respondents, was not intended to continue the status quo of the Sheriff retaining the levied funds. That is, the better reading of the agreement is that, once this court issued a substantive decision on a motion to set aside the levy and that decision was not reversed by a writ or similar expedited application to the court of appeal, the funds held by the Sheriff were to be released pursuant to the decision made by this court on the motion seeking to set aside the levy. This interpretation is supported by the purpose of the stipulation which was not to require that the funds be retained by the Sheriff until the underlying appeal was resolved, but only until either this court or the court of appeal on motion practice resolved the "interim issue" of whether the underlying appeal effected a stay of the attorney's fees judgment. This interpretation is also fully consistent with the statement made by Mr. White at the conclusion of the last hearing when he requested that the stipulation be entered as an order. Petitioner shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the court which may be submitted via e-mail to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For An Order: (A) Recalling And Quashing Writ Of Execution; (B) Vacating Execution Levy; And (C) Staying The Writ Of Execution
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL A. PAINTER OF THE ISSACMAN, KAUFMAN & PAINTER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-310-881-6800 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ROBERT E. WHITE, TEL. NO. 1-415-788-6151 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER EDITIONS LIMITED WEST, INC.'S Motion For An Order: (A) Recalling And Quashing Writ Of Execution; (B) Vacating Execution Levy; And (C) Staying The Writ Of Execution - Edition Limited West's motion to recall and quash writ of execution, vacate execution levy and stay execution of the attorneys' fees and costs judgment is granted. Although there is no authority on the precise issue presented by this motion, in the context of automatic stay of money judgments, there is no compelling reason for a different rule to be applied to a fees and costs judgment confirming an arbitration award and a fees and costs judgment from a trial court order. Even after the California Supreme Court's decision in Bank of San Pedro, Nielsen remains good law. (Chapala Management Corp. v. Stanton (2010) 186 Cal. App. 4th 1532, 1542-47). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jul-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael A. Painter, of Isaacman, Kaufman & Painter, Tel. 323.782.7700, for petitioners, appearing via CourtCall;
Darrell A. Fruth, of Brooks, Pierce, McLendon, Humphrey & Leonard, LLP, Tel. 336.271.3199, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PETITIONER EDITIONS LIMITED WEST, INC.'S Motion To Tax Costs is granted. No timely opposition filed. Respondent did not file his opposition brief until June 30, 2015, one week after it was due. (See CCP sec. 1005(b).) Additionally, the proof of service indicates that the document was served by mail from North Carolina on June 30, 2015. CCP sec. 1005(c) provides that all opposing papers shall be served in a manner "reasonably calculated to ensure delivery to the other party or parties not later than the close of the next business day." Respondent did not serve the opposition in compliance with this statute. Order signed in open court. Request for travel expenses are also denied. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award, Response In Opposition To Petition To Correct
Off Calendar Jan-26-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL A. PAINTER of Isaacman, Kaufman & Painter, A.P.C., Tel No. (310) 881-6800 appearing for the Petitioner Editions Limited West, Inc.;
Atty. DARRELL A. FRUTH of Brooks Pierce, Tel No. (919) 573-6215 appearing for the Respondents Somerset Studios Incorporated and Stephen West.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS SOMERSET STUDIOS INCORPORATED AND STEPHEN WEST'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award, Response In Opposition To Petition To Correct is granted, as no opposition was filed, and there are no grounds upon which to correct or vacate the award. The Petition to Correct the award is not properly before the Court, and furthermore the assertions made therein lack merit. First, the Arbitrator's decision to award certain costs to Respondent is not what is encompassed by miscalculation or mistake pursuant to CCP Sec. 1286.6. Additionally, the language in the Final Award ordering certain fees and costs to be "borne as incurred" was related only to Petitioner's claims against Respondent and thus is not a mistake or miscalculation. Second, the Arbitrator did not exceed her powers in awarding costs and fees only to Respondent. The arbitration agreement provided that the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover fees and costs. Thus, the arbitrator's decision not to award fees or costs to Petitioner cannot be corrected by the Court. (See Moore v. First Bank of San Luis Obispo (2000) 22 Cal.4th 782, 782 ["[w]here the entitlement of a party to attorney fees ... is within the scope of the issues submitted for binding arbitration, the arbitrators do not 'exceed ... their powers' ... by denying the party's request for fees, even where such a denial would be reversible legal error if made by a court in civil litigation."].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)
